files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keda-2581-kafka-partitions-per-pod-scaling",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keda: Kafka Partitions Per Pod Scaling",
+    "description": "Kafka Partitions Per Pod Scaling. Requested by 10+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current keda deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your keda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
helm list -n keda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keda installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review keda configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant keda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get configmap -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keda
```
### Proposal

Enhance the Kafka Scaler or create a new KEDA Scaler to support scaling Kafka by partitions per pod as opposed to simple increase in pod count.

### Use-Case

Say I have 12 partitions for my topic."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Kafka Partitions Per Pod Scaling",
+        "description": "Issue: https://github.com/kedacore/keda/issues/2581
Enhance the Kafka Scaler or create a new KEDA Scaler to support scaling Kafka by partitions per pod as opposed to simple increase in pod count.

Relates to #

QA:
Tested this locally by pushing an image on a local kind cluster. Kafka consumer and
```yaml

+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get events -n keda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Kafka Partitions Per Pod Scaling" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Issue: https://github.com/kedacore/keda/issues/2581
Enhance the Kafka Scaler or create a new KEDA Scaler to support scaling Kafka by partitions per pod as opposed to simple increase in pod count.

Relates to #

QA:
Tested this locally by pushing an image on a local kind cluster.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "4: 1, 2, 4
  6: 1, 2, 3, 6
  8: 1, 2, 4, 8

240: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 15, 16, 20, 24, 30, 40, 48, 60, 8",
+        "def divs(x: Int): Seq[Int] = (1 to x).filter(x % _ == 0)

val res = (1 to 360).map(x => x -> divs(x))

var c = 0

val resF = for {
  i <- 4 to 360
  ds = divs(i)
  if ds.size >= c
  _ = c = ds.size
} yield i -> ds

for {
  (i, ds) <- resF
} {
  println(f"$i%3d: ${ds.mkString(", ")}")
}"
